name: wake-word
description: Voice control for your editor with customizable wake word. Fully local, zero config.

wakeWord.routes.description: Wake phrase routing table. Each entry maps a spoken phrase to an editor command. If empty, default routes are used (Hey Copilot, Hey Claude, Computer).

oneOf.description: A single spoken phrase to listen for (e.g. "hey claude")
Multiple phrases that trigger the same command (e.g. ["hey claude", "open claude"])

phrase.description: Spoken phrase(s) to listen for. Use a string for one phrase or an array for multiple aliases.

cooldownSeconds.description: Override the global cooldown for this route (seconds). Falls back to wakeWord.cooldownSeconds if not set.

wakeWord.cooldownSeconds.description: Seconds to pause wake word listening after handing off to an assistant.

wakeWord.enableOnStartup.description: Start listening automatically when the editor opens.

wakeWord.showNotificationOnDetection.description: Show a brief notification when a wake phrase is detected.

wakeWord.pauseOnFocusLoss.description: Pause wake word listening when the editor loses focus and resume when it regains focus.

wakeWord.confidenceThreshold.description: Minimum confidence score (0.1 to 0.9) required to trigger a wake phrase. Lower values detect more but increase false positives.

wakeWord.nodePath.description: Path to the Node.js executable. Leave empty to auto-detect. Set this if Wake Word cannot find Node.js (common with nvm or fnm). macOS and Linux only.

enumDescriptions: Use the best engine for your platform (Windows Speech on Windows, sherpa-onnx elsewhere).
Force Windows built-in System.Speech engine (Windows only).
Force sherpa-onnx keyword spotting engine (cross-platform, requires model download).
wakeWord.engine.description: Speech engine to use. Leave on 'auto' unless testing a specific engine.

scripts.publish:all: npm run publish:vscode && npm run publish:openvsx
scripts.release: npm run compile && npm run package
